provisions.description: Funds made available under each heading in Title III shall only be used for the purposes specifically described under that heading, notwithstanding any other provision of law.
provisions.title: III
provisions.raw_text: Notwithstanding any other provision of law, funds made available under each heading in this title shall only be used for the purposes specifically desc
notes: Restricts DHS Title III funds to described purposes only

provisions.description: Amends Division A of the Consolidated Appropriations Act, 2019 (P.L. 116-6) by adding SEC. 541, which extends the DHS SAFETY Act prototype project authorities under section 831 of the Homeland Security Act of 2002 from September 30, 2017 to September 30, 2019, and authorizes the Secretary of Homeland Security to carry out prototype projects under 10 U.S.C. 2371b and use the nontraditional government contractor definition.
provisions.title: III
provisions.raw_text: Division A of the Consolidated Appropriations Act, 2019 (Public Law 116–6) is amended by adding after section 540 the following:
notes: Adds SEC. 541 to P.L. 116-6 Division A
Extends DHS SAFETY Act authorities from Sept 30, 2017 to Sept 30, 2019
Grants Secretary of Homeland Security prototype project authorities under 10 U.S.C. 2371b

cross_references.target: Section 831 of the Homeland Security Act of 2002 (6 U.S.C. 391)

provisions.description: Funds provided in this Act under CBP Operations and Support for facilities shall not be available until CBP establishes policies and training programs ensuring facilities adhere to the National Standards on Transport, Escort, Detention, and Search (October 2015). Requires a detailed report within 90 days on implementation of such policies and training.
provisions.account_name: U.S. Customs and Border Protection—Operations and Support
provisions.title: III
provisions.raw_text: None of the funds provided in this Act under ''U.S. Customs and Border Protection—Operations and Support'' for facilities shall be available until U.S.
notes: Conditions facility funds on compliance with TEDS standards
Requires report to appropriations committees, Senate Judiciary Committee, and House Judiciary Committee within 90 days

provisions.description: Requires the Secretary of Homeland Security to report within 30 days on the number of CBP Officers assigned to northern border land ports of entry and temporarily assigned to the humanitarian crisis, including resources needed to restore northern border staffing levels, number of officers temporarily assigned to southwest border, duration, affected ports, and mitigation efforts.
provisions.title: III
provisions.raw_text: No later than 30 days after the date of enactment of this Act, the Secretary of Homeland Security shall provide a report on the number of U.S. Customs
notes: Report must reference June 12, 2018 DHS Northern Border Strategy staffing commitments

cross_references.description: Baseline for northern border staffing levels

provisions.description: Prohibits use of funds from this Act or Division A of P.L. 116-6 for DHS to relocate Trusted Traveler Program vetting from existing locations to the National Targeting Center unless specifically authorized by a statute enacted after the date of enactment of this Act.
provisions.title: III
provisions.raw_text: None of the funds appropriated or otherwise made available by this Act or division A of the Consolidated Appropriations Act, 2019 (Public Law 116–6) fo
notes: Applies to both this Act and P.L. 116-6 Division A funds for DHS
Requires subsequent statutory authorization to relocate TTP vetting

provisions.description: Authorizes deployment of personnel, supplies, or equipment of any DHS component to support activities related to the significant rise in aliens at the southwest border, immigration/customs enforcement, detention and removals, and investigations without reimbursement as jointly agreed by the detailing components.
provisions.title: III
provisions.raw_text: The personnel, supplies, or equipment of any component of the Department of Homeland Security may be deployed to support activities of the Department o
notes: Enables cross-component deployment within DHS without reimbursement

provisions.raw_text: of the amounts provided under this heading, $5,000,000 shall be transferred to ''Office of the Secretary—Office of Inspector General'' and shall remain
notes: Mandatory transfer from Refugee and Entrant Assistance to HHS OIG
For oversight of activities supported with funds appropriated under the REA heading
Remains available until expended

provisions.description: The Secretary of HHS shall prioritize use of community-based residential care (including long-term and transitional foster care and small group homes) and shelter care other than large-scale institutional shelter facilities to house unaccompanied alien children. The Secretary shall prioritize State-licensed and hard-sided dormitories.
provisions.title: IV
provisions.raw_text: The Secretary of Health and Human Services (the ''Secretary'') shall prioritize use of community-based residential care (including long-term and transit
notes: Establishes placement priorities for UAC: community-based care over large-scale institutional facilities
State-licensed and hard-sided dormitories preferred

provisions.description: ORR shall ensure that its grantees and, to the greatest extent practicable, potential sponsors of unaccompanied alien children are aware of current law regarding the use of information collected as part of the sponsor suitability determination process.
provisions.title: IV
provisions.raw_text: The Office of Refugee Resettlement shall ensure that its grantees and, to the greatest extent practicable, potential sponsors of unaccompanied alien ch
notes: Relates to concerns about information sharing between ORR and immigration enforcement affecting sponsor willingness

provisions.description: Prohibits use of funds from this or any prior appropriations Act to reverse changes in procedures made by ORR operational directives issued December 18, 2018, March 23, 2019, and June 10, 2019 regarding the April 13, 2018 MOA on Information Sharing. The Secretary may make changes only upon determining they are necessary to prevent UAC from being placed in danger, with written justification to Congress and HHS IG. The IG shall assess within 15 days whether changes are necessary.
provisions.title: IV
provisions.raw_text: (a) None of the funds provided by this or any prior appropriations Act may be used to reverse changes in procedures made by operational directives issu
notes: Three subsections: (a) prohibition, (b) exception for child safety with written justification, (c) IG assessment within 15 days
Relates to MOA on Information Sharing executed April 13, 2018 between ORR and ICE

provisions.description: Prohibits obligating funds under Refugee and Entrant Assistance to any grantee or contractor to house UAC in a facility not State-licensed, except temporarily due to influx or emergency, subject to detailed conditions on staffing ratios, compliance requirements, monitoring, waivers, and congressional briefing.
provisions.title: IV
provisions.raw_text: None of the funds made available in this Act under the heading ''Department of Health and Human Services—Administration for Children and Families—Refug
notes: Complex provision with 6 conditions for unlicensed facilities operating more than 6 months
Staffing ratios: 1:8 waking, 1:16 sleeping
Max 4 consecutive 60-day waivers per facility
Requires compliance with Flores Settlement Agreement Exhibit 1 requirements
ORR must adhere to section 5.5 of Policies and Procedures Guide (as of May 15, 2019)
Minimum one comprehensive monitoring visit in first 3 months for facilities operating 3+ months
ORR briefing to appropriations committees within 60 days

cross_references.target: Section 462(g)(2) of the Homeland Security Act of 2002 (6 U.S.C. 279(g)(2))

provisions.description: Requires the Secretary to notify appropriations committees at least 15 days before operationalizing an unlicensed facility, specifying whether it is hard- or soft-sided and providing analysis that UAC would otherwise remain in DHS custody over 72 hours or be placed in danger. Within 60 days and monthly thereafter, the Secretary must report on children in care, average length of stay, and details for any child at facility over 60 days.
deadlines: 15 days before operationalizing an unlicensed facility
Within 60 days of bringing facility online, and monthly thereafter
provisions.title: IV
provisions.raw_text: In addition to the existing Congressional notification for formal site assessments of potential influx facilities, the Secretary shall notify the Commi
notes: Supplements existing congressional notification requirements for influx facilities

provisions.description: The Secretary shall ensure, when feasible, that no UAC is placed at an unlicensed facility if the child: (1) is not expected to be placed with a sponsor within 30 days; (2) is under age 13; (3) does not speak English or Spanish as preferred language; (4) has known special needs, behavioral health, or medical issues; (5) is a pregnant or parenting teen; or (6) would have diminished legal services. ORR must notify a child's attorney of record in advance of any transfer.
provisions.title: IV
provisions.raw_text: (a) The Secretary shall ensure that, when feasible, no unaccompanied alien child is at an unlicensed facility if the child—
notes: Six categories of children who should not be at unlicensed facilities when feasible
Subsection (b) requires advance notice to attorney of record for transfers

provisions.description: Prohibits use of funds in this Act to prevent a U.S. Senator or Member of the House from entering any facility used to house unaccompanied alien children for the purpose of conducting oversight, provided the visit is coordinated with ORR at least two business days in advance.
provisions.title: IV
provisions.raw_text: None of the funds made available in this Act may be used to prevent a United States Senator or Member of the House of Representatives from entering, fo
notes: Ensures congressional access to UAC detention facilities
Requires 2 business days advance coordination to avoid interference with operations

cross_references.target: Section 462(g)(2) of the Homeland Security Act of 2002 (6 U.S.C. 279(g)(2))

provisions.description: Requires the Secretary to submit to appropriations committees and make publicly available online, within 14 days and monthly thereafter, a report on children separated from parents/legal guardians by DHS, classified as UAC, and transferred to ORR custody, including: (1) number and ages by sector; (2) documented cause of separation as reported by DHS.
provisions.title: IV
provisions.raw_text: Not later than 14 days after the date of enactment of this Act, and monthly thereafter, the Secretary shall submit to the Committees on Appropriations
notes: Applies regardless of whether separation was pursuant to an option selected by the children, parents, or guardians
Report must be publicly available online
Relates to family separation policy concerns

provisions.description: Funds made available under Refugee and Entrant Assistance in this Act shall be subject to the authorities and conditions of section 224 of division A of the Consolidated Appropriations Act, 2019 (P.L. 116-6).
provisions.title: IV
provisions.raw_text: Funds made available in this Act under the heading ''Department of Health and Human Services—Administration for Children and Families—Refugee and Entran
notes: Incorporates by reference the conditions from the FY2019 regular appropriations act

cross_references.target: Section 224 of Division A of P.L. 116-6

provisions.description: Requires the Secretary to submit a detailed spend plan within 30 days, including: existing grants/contracts for permanent and influx facilities with costs/capacity/timelines; costs for community-based residential care expansion; efforts to expand small-scale shelters and foster care; influx facilities being assessed; legal services, child advocates, and post-release service costs; program administration; and average referrals and discharge rate. Plan must be updated every 60 days until funds are expended or expired.
deadlines: every 60 days thereafter until funds expended or expired
provisions.title: IV
provisions.raw_text: Not later than 30 days after the date of enactment of this Act, the Secretary shall submit to the Committees on Appropriations of the House of Represen
notes: Comprehensive spend plan requirement for the $2.88B REA appropriation

provisions.description: Each amount appropriated or made available by this Act is in addition to amounts otherwise appropriated for the fiscal year involved.
provisions.title: V
provisions.raw_text: Each amount appropriated or made available by this Act is in addition to amounts otherwise appropriated for the fiscal year involved.
notes: Standard supplemental boilerplate establishing additive nature of funds

provisions.description: No part of any appropriation in this Act shall remain available for obligation beyond the current fiscal year unless expressly so provided.
provisions.title: V
provisions.raw_text: No part of any appropriation contained in this Act shall remain available for obligation beyond the current fiscal year unless expressly so provided he
notes: Standard appropriations provision limiting availability to current fiscal year unless otherwise stated

provisions.description: Unless otherwise provided, additional amounts appropriated by this Act shall be available under the authorities and conditions applicable to the corresponding appropriations accounts for fiscal year 2019.
provisions.title: V
provisions.raw_text: Unless otherwise provided for by this Act, the additional amounts appropriated by this Act to appropriations accounts shall be available under the auth
notes: Incorporates existing FY2019 authorities and conditions for each account

provisions.description: Each amount designated as an emergency requirement pursuant to section 251(b)(2)(A)(i) of BBEDCA shall be available only if the President subsequently designates all such amounts and transmits such designations to Congress.
provisions.title: V
provisions.raw_text: Each amount designated in this Act by the Congress as being for an emergency requirement pursuant to section 251(b)(2)(A)(i) of the Balanced Budget and
notes: Standard BBEDCA emergency designation provision requiring presidential concurrence

cross_references.target: Section 251(b)(2)(A)(i) of the Balanced Budget and Emergency Deficit Control Act of 1985

provisions.description: Any amount designated as an emergency requirement and subsequently so designated by the President, if transferred pursuant to transfer authorities in this Act, shall retain the emergency designation.
provisions.title: V
provisions.raw_text: Any amount appropriated by this Act, designated by the Congress as an emergency requirement pursuant to section 251(b)(2)(A)(i) of the Balanced Budget
notes: Ensures emergency designation follows transferred funds

cross_references.target: Section 251(b)(2)(A)(i) of the Balanced Budget and Emergency Deficit Control Act of 1985

provisions.description: Requires the Comptroller General (GAO) to submit a report within 180 days on the number of asylum officers, immigration judges (including temporary), and support staff necessary to: (1) fairly and effectively make credible fear determinations for family units and UAC; (2) ensure credible fear determination and asylum interview completed within 20 days for family units; (3) fairly and effectively review appeals of credible fear determinations. Report shall also determine any physical infrastructure needs such as hearing or courtroom space.
provisions.title: V
provisions.raw_text: Not later than 180 days after the date of the enactment of this Act, the Comptroller General of the United States shall submit a report to the Committ
notes: GAO study on staffing and infrastructure needs for credible fear and asylum processes
Three specific areas of inquiry plus physical infrastructure assessment

flagged_issues: Bill has no divisions — only Titles I-V. by_division is empty.
Both CBP and ICE use account name 'Operations and Support' — distinguished by agency field.
REA $2.88B is the largest single appropriation, comprising 63% of total budget authority.
All appropriations carry BBEDCA emergency designation requiring presidential concurrence per SEC. 504.
SEC. 302 amends P.L. 116-6 to extend DHS SAFETY Act authorities — legislative rider on supplemental.
Sub-allocations of REA $866M include further breakdowns: $27M and $185M are sub-sub-allocations.
$5M mandatory transfer from Refugee and Entrant Assistance to HHS OIG for oversight.
